title: Micropolypodium zurquinum
intro: Micropolypodium zurquium is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as a type of fern. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Micropolypodium zurquium include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Micropolypodium zurquium is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into various folk remedies and traditional medicines.
taxonomy: Micropolypodium zurquinum is classified in the family Polypodiaceae, which belongs to the division Pteridophyta. This family is a part of the class Polypodiopsida and is also known as the 'polypody ferns'. The classification further extends to the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum is known as Tracheophyta. The species Micropolypodium zurquinum is native to certain regions and is a type of vascular plant. Its classification helps in understanding its evolutionary history and genetic relationships.
